clean_story:
Now I will teach you gold.

Gold is heavy rock-shine like wet sun on stone—soft as fat, not hard like flint. It bends if you bite, does not break like shell or crack like ice. When fire-lick touches it, color stays bright like morning light, never turns black like burnt wood. You find it in river-bed dirt, heavy in hand, never rusting green like copper or flaking red like blood-stone. If you see bright yellow that gleams far, too heavy for its size—this is gold.
